report_name: Microsoft's Record-Breaking June Patch Tuesday: Over 200 Flaws and Three Zero-Days Patched

malware.name: YellowKey
malware.description: Exploit name associated with the BitLocker Security Feature Bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-50507.

malware.name: GreenPlasma
malware.description: Exploit name associated with the Elevation of Privilege vulnerability in the Windows Collaborative Translation Framework, CVE-2026-45586.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-50507
vulnerabilities.description: A BitLocker Security Feature Bypass vulnerability that could allow an attacker with physical access to access user data. This is believed to be a patch for the 'YellowKey' exploit.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-49160
vulnerabilities.description: A Denial-of-Service (DoS) vulnerability in HTTP.sys, which can be triggered by a specially crafted HTTP/2 request, leading to what researchers call an 'HTTP/2 Bomb' attack against web servers like Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS).

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-45586
vulnerabilities.description: An Elevation of Privilege vulnerability in the Windows Collaborative Translation Framework, suspected to be the fix for the 'GreenPlasma' exploit.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-45657
vulnerabilities.description: A critical, potentially 'wormable' Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability in the Windows Kernel's handling of TCP/IP that could be used for self-propagating attacks.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-47291
vulnerabilities.description: An integer overflow vulnerability in HTTP.sys that allows an unauthenticated, remote attacker to execute arbitrary code.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-44815
vulnerabilities.description: A stack-based buffer overflow in the Windows DHCP Client Service that could be exploited by an attacker on the same network segment to achieve Remote Code Execution (RCE).

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-42897
vulnerabilities.description: A notable vulnerability addressed in the June 2026 Patch Tuesday update.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-45585
vulnerabilities.description: A notable vulnerability addressed in the June 2026 Patch Tuesday update.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Vendor that released the June 2026 Patch Tuesday security updates for its products.

identities.name: Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS)
identities.description: Web server software affected by the HTTP.sys vulnerability CVE-2026-49160.

identities.name: Windows BitLocker
identities.description: Windows disk encryption feature affected by the security feature bypass vulnerability CVE-2026-50507.

identities.name: Windows HTTP.sys
identities.description: Windows component affected by vulnerabilities CVE-2026-49160 and CVE-2026-47291.

identities.name: Windows Collaborative Translation Framework
identities.description: Windows component affected by the elevation of privilege vulnerability CVE-2026-45586.

identities.name: Windows Kernel
identities.description: Core Windows component affected by the wormable RCE vulnerability CVE-2026-45657.

identities.name: Windows DHCP Client Service
identities.description: Windows service affected by the RCE vulnerability CVE-2026-44815.

indicators.name: HTTP.sys Service Crash
indicators.pattern:
title: HTTP.sys Service Crash
id: 9e8a1b4c-7d6e-4f5a-8b3c-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects Windows Event Log entries for HTTP.sys service crashes (Event IDs 15300-15301), which may indicate failed exploitation attempts against CVE-2026-49160 or CVE-2026-47291.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-june-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-3-zero-day-200-flaws/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1499
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: system
detection:
  selection:
    Provider_Name: 'Microsoft-Windows-HttpEvent'
    EventID:
      - 15300
      - 15301
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects Windows Event Log entries for HTTP.sys service crashes (Event IDs 15300-15301), which may indicate failed exploitation attempts against CVE-2026-49160 or CVE-2026-47291.

indicators.name: Anomalous Child Process of LSASS
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Child Process of LSASS
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-1b0a-9e8d7c6b5a4f
status: experimental
description: Detects child processes of lsass.exe, which is highly anomalous. This could indicate credential dumping or other malicious activity, potentially related to exploitation of privilege escalation vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-45586.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-june-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-3-zero-day-200-flaws/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1003
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\lsass.exe'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\werfault.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects child processes of lsass.exe, which is highly anomalous and may indicate exploitation of privilege escalation vulnerabilities such as CVE-2026-45586.
